questions.text: The phase between two successive M phases is called:
questions.explanation: Interphase is the 'resting' phase where the cell prepares for division by growing and replicating DNA.

questions.text: DNA replication occurs during which phase of the cell cycle?
questions.explanation: The Synthesis (S) phase is where DNA is duplicated.

questions.text: During which stage of mitosis do chromosomes align at the equatorial plate?
questions.explanation: Metaphase is characterized by all chromosomes coming to lie at the equator.

questions.text: The stage of mitosis where centromeres split and chromatids move to opposite poles is:
questions.explanation: In Anaphase, sister chromatids separate and move towards opposite poles.

questions.text: Crossing over occurs during which stage of Meiosis?
questions.explanation: Pachytene is the stage of Prophase I where exchange of genetic material happens.

questions.text: The division of the cytoplasm is known as:
questions.explanation: Cytokinesis follows nuclear division (karyokinesis) to complete cell division.

questions.text: In animal cells, cytokinesis involves the formation of a:
questions.explanation: Animal cells divide by the formation of a furrow in the plasma membrane.

questions.text: The pairing of homologous chromosomes is called:
questions.explanation: Synapsis occurs during the Zygotene stage of Prophase I.

questions.text: Cells that do not divide further exit G1 phase and enter an inactive stage called:
questions.explanation: The G0 phase is the quiescent stage where cells remain metabolically active but do not proliferate.

questions.text: How many mitotic divisions are needed to produce 128 cells from a single cell?
questions.explanation: 2^n = 128, so n = 7 divisions.

questions.text: In plants, cytokinesis starts from the center and moves outwards, involving the formation of a:
questions.explanation: The cell plate is formed by the fusion of vesicles from the Golgi apparatus.

questions.text: Synaptonemal complex dissolves during:
questions.explanation: The dissolution of the synaptonemal complex marks the beginning of Diplotene.

questions.text: X-shaped structures seen in Prophase I of meiosis are:
questions.explanation: Chiasmata are the visible sites of crossing over.

questions.text: Mitosis is also called 'equational division' because:
questions.explanation: The daughter cells have the same number of chromosomes as the parent cell.

questions.text: The nuclear envelope reappears during:
questions.explanation: Telophase is essentially the reverse of prophase.

questions.text: Centriole duplication occurs during:
questions.explanation: Centrioles duplicate in the cytoplasm during the S phase, while DNA duplicates in the nucleus.

questions.text: Meiosis results in the production of:
questions.explanation: Meiosis reduces chromosome number by half and produces four daughter cells.

questions.text: The correct sequence of stages in Prophase I is:
questions.explanation: L-Z-P-D-D is the standard chronological order.

questions.text: Chromosome morphology (shape, size) is best studied during:
questions.explanation: Chromosomes are most condensed and clearly visible at the equatorial plate in Metaphase.

questions.text: Small disc-shaped structures at the surface of centromeres are:
questions.explanation: Kinetochores serve as the attachment sites for spindle fibers.

questions.text: Meiosis II is similar to:
questions.explanation: Meiosis II is an equational division where sister chromatids separate, like mitosis.

questions.text: If a cell has 24 chromosomes, after meiosis I it will have ______ chromosomes.
questions.explanation: Meiosis I is a reductional division.

questions.text: The stage between Meiosis I and Meiosis II is called:
questions.explanation: Interkinesis is generally short-lived and there is no DNA replication.

questions.text: Recombination nodules appear during:
questions.explanation: These are the sites where crossing over occurs between non-sister chromatids.

questions.text: Terminallisation of chiasmata occurs in:
questions.explanation: In Diakinesis, the chiasmata shift towards the ends of the chromosomes.

questions.text: What is the significance of mitosis?
questions.explanation: Mitosis ensures identical cells for growth, tissue repair, and replacing dead cells.

questions.text: Colchicine, a drug, arrests mitosis at:
questions.explanation: It inhibits spindle formation, trapping chromosomes at the metaphase plate.

questions.text: In human beings, the duration of the cell cycle is approximately:
questions.explanation: Most mammalian cells divide once every 24 hours.

questions.text: The cell cycle of Yeast takes about:
questions.explanation: Yeast has a relatively rapid cell cycle compared to humans.

questions.text: What happens in G2 phase?
questions.explanation: Proteins like tubulin are synthesized during G2 to prepare for the M phase.

questions.text: Karyokinesis refers to:
questions.explanation: Standard nuclear division phase of mitosis.

questions.text: Disappearance of nucleolus and nuclear membrane starts in:
questions.explanation: By the end of prophase, these organelles are no longer visible.

questions.text: Bivalents or tetrads are formed during:
questions.explanation: Pairing of homologous chromosomes forms bivalents.

questions.text: Centromere is the part of chromosome where ______ are attached.
questions.explanation: Specifically at the kinetochore region of the centromere.

questions.text: Meiosis I is called reductional division because:
questions.explanation: It reduces the cell from diploid (2n) to haploid (n).

questions.text: Significance of meiosis is:
questions.explanation: Meiosis creates new combinations through crossing over and ensures fertilization returns the organism to diploidy.

questions.text: Syncytium is formed when:
options: Karyokinesis is not followed by cytokinesis
questions.explanation: Liquid endosperm in coconut is a common example of a multinucleate syncytium.

questions.text: Homologous chromosomes separate, but sister chromatids remain associated at their centromeres during:
questions.explanation: This is a key difference between Meiosis I and Mitosis/Meiosis II.

questions.text: The amount of DNA (C) doubles in S phase, but ______ remains the same.
questions.explanation: Even though DNA content becomes 4C from 2C, chromosome number does not increase.

questions.text: Which stage marks the end of meiosis I?
questions.explanation: Telophase I is followed by a short stage called interkinesis before meiosis II begins.
